title: Timing, Resource Graphs, Transfer, Caching, and User-Performance Boundaries
description: The audit calls server response time page speed, double-counts cached resources, ignores compression and render work, and reports one lab run as user experience. Learners cumulatively produce bounded page-resource and timing audit through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Separate queue, DNS, connect, TLS, request, first-byte, body, parse, render, and total timing with clock and instrumentation limits.
Classify document, stylesheet, script, image, font, media, API, iframe, preload, and redirect resources with initiator and origin.
Reconcile compressed transfer, decoded body, cached reuse, duplicate URLs, variant keys, and large-resource policy.
